title: Behavioral-laziness: least-action regularization of transfer-operator trajectories
description:
Self-contained numpy core and synthetic reproduction accompanying the paper "Recovering the transfer operator of a multicluster open system from aggregate data: the least-action principle on its trajectory" (informal name: "Behavioral Laziness").

Recovering a transfer operator from two aggregate snapshots is severely under-determined, so the solution set is an affine manifold on which minimum-norm (Tikhonov) regularization is not neutral — it underestimates self-retention. This package implements the proposed least-action prior on the operator trajectory: null-space projection (hard, preserves the observed macro increment A·P exactly) and EWMA (soft), together with entropic optimal-transport baselines (Sinkhorn), an applicability phase map (delta × rho), and causal inner-CV selection of the regularization strength λ. All demos are deterministic (seeded) and CPU-only. Domain runs (labour market, radiation-damped relativistic-electron ensemble) depend on closed data and separate solver packages and are not included in this archive.
